_note: Pricing basis for cost estimates in report.py. Rates are the pay-as-you-go pre-recorded/batch tier we actually call, in USD per audio-hour unless unit says otherwise, dated 2026-07-15 and consistent with blog part one. Cost cannot be read back from most provider APIs, so report.py estimates it from audio duration processed (from _meta.json) times these rates, applying min_seconds_per_job where a per-job minimum inflates short-clip cost. 'path' flags where the API we use is priced differently from the provider's cheapest tier.

rev.note: 15s per-job minimum assumed; verify against invoice

amazon-transcribe.note: we use the streaming API (no sync pre-recorded REST) = $0.01/min = $0.60/hr; batch is $0.006/min (~$0.36/hr) but one job per clip. Earlier 1.44 was a stale $0.024/min rate.

google-chirp.note: sync recognize; dynamic-batch tier is ~0.18/hr but a different, slower API

gemini-2.5-flash.note: LLM transcription, token-billed; report.py uses captured usageMetadata when present, else estimates input tokens from audio seconds x 32

fish-audio-asr.note: duration rounded up to the nearest second

cartesia-ink-whisper.note: Batch endpoint (/stt) is 1 credit per 2s of audio = 0.5 credit/s x 3600 = 1800 credits/hr x $0.00005/credit (Pro $5/100k) = $0.09/hr. Real-time websocket STT is double, 1 credit/s = $0.18/hr. Silence is billed. The earlier 0.54 (3 credits/s) was a misread of the console and is wrong.

inworld-stt-1.note: On-Demand/free rate; drops to 0.10/hr on paid tiers. Free tier includes 400 minutes.
